topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The click of the laptop closing echoed in the quiet room. Mark leaned back in his chair, rubbing the bridge of his nose. He’d been waiting for Sarah to admit something for weeks—something about the phone she kept tucked under her mattress. Now, after finding the login details scribbled on a sticky note near her textbook, the waiting was over. He clicked. The feed loaded instantly. A cascade of carefully curated photos and cryptic captions filled the screen. A tightening sensation gripped his chest. He scrolled, his jaw clenching. It was a world he hadn't known existed, a hidden dimension behind the polite veneer of his daughter.

[story 2]

"Just tell me what this is," David demanded, pacing the hallway. He’d been hovering by the door for nearly an hour, the silence of the house amplifying the drumming in his ears. When Sarah finally sighed, dropping her phone onto the sofa cushion with a defeated thud, David snatched it up. The profile was jarringly different from the one she presented to him. He navigated through the posts, his eyes scanning rapidly, a restless energy buzzing beneath his skin. Every new picture brought a fresh wave of urgency. He felt a distinct need to know the context, to understand the whole picture before she could shut it down again.

[story 3]

I kept checking the notifications. Every time I walked into the kitchen, my gaze drifted toward the tablet she was ostensibly using for homework. My hands felt restless, tapping a quick, uneven rhythm on the countertop. There it was—a username I didn't recognize, a stream of activity I couldn't ignore. A knot formed in my stomach; this was a breach, a secret she was actively guarding. I moved closer, my breath catching slightly as I read a comment. It was clear, undeniable. The need to confront this hidden space, to force an explanation out of her, was becoming overwhelming.
